title: Simple Play Kitchen Sink and Stove
summary: One base cabinet, two personalities — build it as an open-front sink stand or add a side-swing door and acrylic "glass" to turn the same box into a play stove.
description:
This is one small cabinet with two build paths from the same starting point: a basic 1x12 base with a backsplash and countertop is a sink stand as soon as you add a bowl; add a hinged door with an acrylic window and it becomes a stove. Build the base identically either way, then branch at the very last step.

The acrylic "glass" in the stove door is genuinely worth the small extra cost and care — kids can see there's nothing dangerous behind it, which matters more for a play stove than for almost any other pretend-play piece. Whether you glue it in or screw it, the plan is specific about one thing: if using screws, make sure they have NO bevel on the underside of the head. A beveled screw head will crack acrylic as it's driven, where a flat pan-head screw won't.

The door hinge choice is a real safety decision, not a style one: a side-swing door, not a lift-up or top-hinge door. Side-swing keeps small fingers and toes away from a door that could otherwise drop on them, which is exactly the kind of small mechanical choice that matters more in a toy built for a toddler than in almost any adult furniture piece.

A scrap plywood or beadboard back panel is optional but recommended — it stiffens the whole assembly against the wobble that an open-backed cabinet this light would otherwise have with a kid playing at it enthusiastically.

steps.title: Attach the aprons
steps.body: Attach the 1x2 toe-kick apron and the 1x4 front-of-sink apron to the two side panels — 1-1/4" brad nails and glue.

steps.title: Add the bottom shelf
steps.body: Add the bottom shelf, fastening it to both the sides and the 1x2 apron. It can sit at any height that works for your build.

steps.title: Add the top and backsplash
steps.body: Attach the backsplash to the countertop first, then attach the whole assembled top to the base.

steps.title: Build the sink door (or stop here for a sink)
steps.body:
Build the door with 3/4" pocket holes and 1-1/4" pocket hole screws. Attach the acrylic glass to the back of the door frame with craft glue or hot glue, or predrill and use screws — if using screws, make sure they have NO bevel on the underside of the head, or the acrylic will crack as the screw seats.

This completes the sink base — accessorize with a sink bowl, curtains, and a play faucet as you like.

steps.title: Convert to a stove
steps.body: To make the base into a play stove, add the built door with SIDE-SWING hinges rather than a top or lift-up hinge — side-swing keeps small fingers and toes safely away from the door. Install a magnetic clasp to keep the door closed, then add a handle and four knobs to the front for the "burners."

steps.title: Add the optional back and finish
steps.body: Nail on the optional plywood or beadboard back panel — it stiffens the whole cabinet against wobble during enthusiastic play. Sand and finish with kid-safe paint.

images.alt: Simple wooden play kitchen sink and stove base for kids
